events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario involves PPE selection where voltage glove class is wrong or overdue. This is a safety-critical issue.

  "findings": [
    "Voltage glove class is wrong for the energized work voltage level or gloves are past required test date",
    "Insulating gloves not matched to NFPA 70E arc-flash/shock hazard category for the task voltage",
    "Gloves overdue for dielectric testing per OSHA 1910.137 / 1910 Subpart I requirements"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop energized work immediately until correct-class, in-test-date gloves are provided",
    "Verify maximum exposure voltage and select appropriate glove class (Class 0 up to 1kV, Class 1 up to 7.5kV, etc.)",
    "Inspect glove test labels/dates; remove from service if overdue or failed air test",
    "Reissue compliant voltage-rated gloves with leather protectors and confirm arc-rated PPE layering"
  ],
  "rationale": "Wrong-class or overdue voltage gloves provide inadequate or invalid electrical insulation, exposing the worker to shock and arc-flash hazards. NFPA 70E and OSHA 1910 Subpart I require voltage-rated gloves matched to the exposure voltage and within current dielectric test intervals; non-compliant PPE is a critical defect requiring work stoppage.",
  "references": [
    "NFPA 70E",
    "OSHA 1910 Subpart I",
    "OSHA 1910.137"
  ]
}
